questions.question: If a prospect cannot provide a metric for success ('We don't track that'), what should you do?

options.text: Co-author a metric/estimate with them based on industry standards
Ask them to research it and hang up

questions.explanation: If the metric doesn't exist, you help them build it. 'If you had to guess, would it be X or Y?' This establishes the baseline for ROI.

questions.question: What is the 'Litmus Test' for a Champion?

options.text: They take a specific action to advance the deal (e.g., getting you access to the Economic Buyer)

questions.explanation: Influence requires action. If they won't or can't do work for you internally, they are not a Champion; they are just a Coach.

questions.question: Why ask 'Whose budget will this come out of?' instead of 'Are you the buyer?'

options.text: It is more polite and reveals the organizational structure (Economic Buyer)

questions.explanation: Directly asking authority challenges ego. Asking about budget source reveals the P&L owner naturally.

questions.question: Which question uncovers Decision Criteria?
questions.explanation: Decision Criteria are the specific rules they use to judge you. Knowing the 'Must Haves' prevents you from losing on a technicality.

questions.question: Why do deals 'slip' past the quarter end?

options.text: Because the salesperson worked too hard
Because the Decision Process (Legal/Security/Procurement) was not mapped or accounted for

questions.explanation: Process steps take time. If you didn't ask 'Does this need a security review?' and account for the 3 weeks it takes, your deal slips.
